name: Winston Churchill
title: Prime Minister and Minister of Defence

red_lines: Negotiated peace or capitulation to Nazi Germany
Soviet domination of all of post-war Europe
core_narratives: British resolve and the spirit of the Blitz
Preservation of the British Empire and Commonwealth

decision_history.event_description: France fell and Britain stood alone against Nazi Germany
decision_history.decision_taken: Rejected Halifax's peace overtures, committed to fighting on regardless of odds
decision_history.outcome: Britain survived the Blitz and maintained resistance until allies joined the war

decision_history.event_description: Germany invaded the Soviet Union in Operation Barbarossa
decision_history.decision_taken: Immediately offered alliance and material support to Stalin despite lifelong anti-communism
decision_history.outcome: Grand Alliance formed; Eastern Front drained majority of German military strength
context_factors: need to relieve pressure on Britain

decision_history.event_description: Strategic bombing debate over how to strike back at Germany
decision_history.decision_taken: Authorized area bombing campaign targeting German industrial cities and civilian morale
decision_history.outcome: Massive destruction of German cities; controversial civilian casualties; debatable strategic impact
context_factors: limited options for striking Germany directly
bomber offensive as second front substitute

cultural_context.domestic_power_base: Conservative Party (wartime coalition), military establishment, monarchy support, popular wartime mandate

military_posture.force_disposition: defensive posture transitioning to offensive capability with American support

ongoing_conflicts: Defense of Empire possessions in Asia
